projectDescription: A minimalist line editor written in C, inspired by the classic Unix ed, offering simple line-based text editing from the command line.
keyFeatures: Read and write files to an in-memory line buffer
Print lines by number or range (p command)
Delete lines by number or range (d command)
Append and insert text interactively (a and i commands)
Line range addressing with comma-separated syntax (e.g. 2,5p)
Single-file C implementation — easy to read and modify
Zero dependencies beyond the C standard library
Build with a single gcc invocation
publicApiSurface: [N]a      — append text after line N (end with a lone '.')
[N]i      — insert text before line N (end with a lone '.')
